events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes EMT bending & installation at rough-complete stage with "improper coupling engagement" as the observed field condition. This is a defect that needs correction. The work is roughly 70% complete until defects are corrected.

Improper coupling engagement means couplings aren't properly threaded/engaged onto the EMT, which compromises the raceway system's continuity, grounding, and mechanical integrity. This is a non-compliant condition under NEC Art. 358.

Decision: fail (non-compliant work)
Risk: medium (improper coupling engagement could affect grounding continuity and mechanical protection)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: 70
Value: 0 (no specific numeric reading requested)
Confidence: 0.85

Findings: improper coupling engagement on EMT runs, couplings not fully engaged compromising raceway continuity and grounding
Actions: re-engage/replace couplings properly, verify set-screw or compression couplings are fully seated, inspect all coupling points
Rationale: improper coupling engagement violates NEC Art. 358 requirements for secure installation and raceway continuity
References: NEC Art. 358, NEC Ch. 9 (fill)
